title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the game-winning goal 39 seconds into overtime, leading the Boston Bruins to a 2-1 victory over the Los Angeles Kings.
McAvoy, dealing with dental injuries, was hit into the glass during the game, requiring further dental work post-match.
Despite injuries, McAvoy's performance and resilience contribute to his leadership role among the Bruins' alternate captains.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected from the Celtics-Spurs game after receiving two technical fouls before halftime.
Following his ejection, Brown criticized the referees on social media, using strong language to express his frustration.
The Celtics lost 125-116 to the Spurs, missing contributions from Jaylen Brown, who had eight points and seven rebounds.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, sentenced to life in prison with parole possibility after 17 years for murdering Margarita Morehead in 2023.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to second-degree murder, assault with a weapon, and violating a protection order in Chelsea, prosecutors said.
During a May 2023 altercation, Alvarez stabbed Morehead after arguing, then fled but later turned himself in, officials reported.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: MBTA Transit Police seek public help identifying a suspect in an alleged assault on Feb. 27 at Prudential station.
The suspect allegedly spat and struck a Green Line rider multiple times at around 8 p.m.
Photos show the suspect wearing black clothing with tattoos on neck and hands; tips can be submitted anonymously.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots signed wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal worth up to $80 million.
New England remains interested in trading for Eagles receiver A.J. Brown despite signing Doubs.
The Eagles reportedly seek a 2027 first-round and a 2026 second-round pick for Brown.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, from Fort Lauderdale, Florida, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team Ellie.
Weisman is inspired by her mother's battle with breast cancer and the support of the Ellie Fund.
The Ellie Fund provides crucial support services like transportation assistance and meals for breast cancer patients.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police responded to bomb threats at Stop & Shop locations in Jamaica Plain and Allston on Monday.
Reading police also investigated a bomb threat at a Stop & Shop on Walkers Brook Drive, evacuated as a precaution.
No suspicious devices were found, but investigations are ongoing to determine if the threats were related.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots WR Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signed a four-year contract worth $68 million, potentially $80 million, with the New England Patriots.
Doubs recorded 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns for the Green Bay Packers in the 2025 season.
Doubs is noted for his red-zone efficiency, securing 18 touchdowns over three seasons with the Packers.

title: Southbridge woman dies after being rescued from house fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A Southbridge woman in her 60s died after being rescued from a house fire on South St. Monday.
Firefighters found no working smoke alarms in the home, with the fire suspected to have started in the kitchen.
The woman's death is the eighth this year in Massachusetts homes lacking functional smoke alarms.

title: New ‘call a boomer’ phone line connects young people in Boston to seniors in Nevada
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matter Neuroscience launches 'call a boomer' phone line connecting Boston youth and Reno seniors via recorded conversations.
The project aims to bridge generational divides, enhancing connectivity and happiness despite age and political differences.
Initial success in San Francisco and Abilene led to this initiative, with a trial phone placed near Boston University.
